hypothesis: If a two-stage MD+GCMC simulation pipeline with DFT-refined polymer–CO2–H2O force-field parameters is coupled to Bayesian optimization using polymer-specific molecular embeddings, then higher-CO2-uptake polyamines at 298 K and 0.4 mbar will be discovered within a limited simulation budget (e.g., 30 evaluations) more efficiently than with random search or BO using general embeddings, because refined physics improves label accuracy and the surrogate exploits polymer structure–adsorption relationships.
